title: Humboltia warmingii
intro: Humboldtia warmingii, also known as Kavala or Kavalam, is a medicinal plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia warmingii include alkaloids, flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Humboldtia warmingii is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is used to make herbal preparations such as a cough syrup, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory paste.
